content.problemStatement:
Vanja and Miksi have already finished everything for this year at their university, so they decided to spend their free time playing a game with a binary sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ (i.e. a sequence containing only integers 000 and 111).

At the beginning of the game, Vanja and Miksi write down the expression ∗A1∗A2∗A3∗…∗AN* A_1 * A_2 * A_3 * \ldots * A_N∗A1​∗A2​∗A3​∗…∗AN​ (note below that each ∗*∗ is a placeholder and does not denote multiplication).

The players take turns alternately, starting with Vanja. The game lasts NNN turns. In each turn, the current player must replace the first (leftmost) remaining ∗*∗ by the sign +++ or −-− (or, equivalently, by the operation of addition or subtraction).

After the last turn, the boys calculate the resulting value VVV of the expression. If ∣V∣≥K|V| \ge K∣V∣≥K, the winner is Vanja; otherwise, the winner is Miksi.

Please predict the winner of the game if both players play optimally.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and K. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — 1 if Vanja (the first player) is the winner or 2 if Miksi (the second player) is the winner.

sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case, the sum of the binary sequence is 1, which is greater than or equal to K (1). Therefore, Vanja wins. In the second test case, the sum of the binary sequence is 1, which is less than K (5). Therefore, Miksi wins.

solution.approach: To determine the winner of the game, we need to consider the optimal strategy for both players. The key observation is that the first player (Vanja) can always make a move that maximizes the absolute value of the final expression, while the second player (Miksi) tries to minimize it. We can use a greedy approach to determine the winner by simulating the game and keeping track of the maximum absolute value that can be achieved.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum length of the binary sequence.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the maximum length of the binary sequence.

code.Python3:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, K = map(int, input().split())
    A = list(map(int, input().split()))
    sum_A = sum(A)
    if sum_A >= K:
        print(1)
    else:
        print(2)
